title: start_registry: wait for registry to be ready
body:
The usual 'podman run -d' race condition: we've been forking
off the container but not actually making sure it's up; this
leads to flakes in which we try (and fail) to access it.

Solution: use curl to check the port; we will expect a zero
exit status once we can connect. Time out at ten seconds.

Resolves: #675

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: @mtrmac this one avoids the 10-second wait; ptal

Thanks!

Yes, that’s much nicer than #676. LGTM pending tests.

Side note, simply for the record: I considered adding `&>/dev/null` to the curl invocation, because it's possible that a test will fail and the 'connection refused' in the logs might lead the troubleshooter down the wrong path. I decided to leave the output just in case there's an error other than 'connection refused', and I decided not to add a 'echo registry container is up' message before `return` because, noisy.
